commit.message:
Fix aspire start failing in VS Code integrated terminal (#15980)

* Fix aspire start failing in VS Code integrated terminal

When 'aspire start' runs from the VS Code Aspire terminal, the detached
child process inherits ASPIRE_EXTENSION_* environment variables, causing
it to detect extension mode and delegate back to the extension via
StartDebugSessionAsync instead of launching the AppHost. This results in
an immediate exit with code 0.

Fix:
- Strip extension-related env vars from the detached child process in
  DetachedProcessLauncher (Unix: remove from startInfo.Environment,
  Windows: build filtered environment block for CreateProcessW)
- Add --non-interactive guard in RunCommand to skip extension delegation
  when running as a child of 'aspire start'
- Set isExtensionHost=false in StartCommand so Dashboard URL always
  shows in 'aspire start' output
- Fix column width calculation in RenderAppHostSummary to only include
  labels that are actually displayed
- Add missing KnownConfigNames constants for extension env vars

Fixes #15786

* Fix detached child env filtering

Preserve DEBUG_SESSION_* values when the detached child CLI is launched from the VS Code Aspire terminal so the AppHost keeps IDE execution and dashboard debug integration. Also address follow-up review issues in StartCommand and the Windows filtered environment block builder.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: prefix-based env var filter, remove unused usings

* Restore using Aspire.Cli.Utils needed for ICliUpdateNotifier

* Add context comment explaining why isExtensionHost is always false

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: mode mismatch when switching models in USD/PLN display

When ScenarioEditor was in fixed-value mode (USD/PLN) and the user
switched prediction models, the useEffect that syncs localValues from
the new scenarios always wrote raw percentage deltas regardless of
display mode.  This caused percentage values (e.g. -15) to appear as
USD prices, and toDelta() to compute deltas like -107.5%, producing
nonsensical results.

The fix converts percentage deltas to the current display mode (absolute
USD/PLN prices) when syncing from an external scenarios change, using
the same conversion logic as toggleStockMode/toggleFxMode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: strict build/test gates — never commit broken builds or failing tests

Build Gate (EngineerAgentBase):
- CommitViaLocalWorkspaceAsync now returns bool (false = blocked)
- When build fails after MaxBuildRetries (now 5), tries full code
  regeneration from scratch with fresh AI call
- If regeneration also fails, reverts workspace and blocks the commit

- No broken build ever reaches GitHub

Test Gate (EngineerAgentBase):
- New TestWithRetryAndRemoveAsync: tries fixing up to MaxTestRetries
  (now 20) attempts
- If tests still fail after all attempts, RemoveFailingTestsAsync asks
  AI to remove unfixable tests with documentation comments
- Removed tests get // TEST REMOVED comments explaining the failure
- Verifies remaining tests pass before allowing commit
- Post-test build verification ensures test fixes don't break the build
- No failing tests ever reach GitHub

Test Gate (TestEngineerAgent):
- Build-gates test PR creation: if tests can't compile, returns -1
  and skips PR creation instead of committing broken test files
- New RemoveFailingTestsForTierAsync: per-tier last-resort test
  removal with recursive cleanup pass
- Test fix reverts on build breakage (was: just break out of loop)

Infrastructure:
- LocalWorkspace.RevertUncommittedChangesAsync(): git checkout + clean
- MaxBuildRetries: 3 → 5 (more chances before regeneration)
- MaxTestRetries: 3 → 20 (aggressive fixing before removal)
- Removed dead TestWithRetryAsync method

commit.message:
fix: auto-connect passthrough tasks (e.g. DebugLogTask) to downstream… (#373)

* fix: auto-connect passthrough tasks (e.g. DebugLogTask) to downstream tasks in workflow

Passthrough tasks like DebugLogTask have `additionalProperties: true` with
no named output properties in their static schema. When followed by another
task with `additionalProperties: true` (e.g. OutputTask), the auto-connect
created zero dataflow connections between them. Inside a MapTask, this left
multiple disconnected ending nodes, causing PROPERTY_ARRAY merge to
double-wrap iteration results in arrays.

Two changes in Workflow.autoConnect and updateBoundaryTaskSchemas:

1. makeMatch: when source has additionalProperties with no named output
   ports and is a passthrough task, infer output port names from its
   incoming dataflows to establish downstream connections.

2. updateBoundaryTaskSchemas: when building OutputTask schema from a
   passthrough source, trace back through the source's incoming dataflows
   to resolve the actual property schema.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01QVJLJN35ct7WL1Fx3sjwg1

* fix: address PR review comments - typed cast, connectedInputKeys guard, and passthrough tests

commit.message:
headlamp: bypass forward-auth for OIDC endpoints

The authentik forward-auth middleware was intercepting /oidc and /oidc-callback
requests, starting its own auth flow before Headlamp's OIDC handler could run.
This created a 5-6 redirect chain that prevented the OIDC token exchange from
completing.

Split IngressRoute into two rules:
- /oidc and /oidc-callback: skip forward-auth (Headlamp handles OIDC natively)
- All other paths: keep forward-auth for access control

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: sloppy-mode TailCall must Return, not Continue

When a TailCall in sloppy mode falls back to handle_call_any_receiver,
the result was DispatchAction::Continue — but TailCall is terminal with
no next bytecode instruction. This caused the interpreter to read past
the function's bytecode, producing wrong results for mutual tail-call
recursion (even/odd test).

Fix: convert Continue to Return so the callee's result propagates
correctly to the caller.

Fixes: test_tail_call_mutual_recursion_sloppy_small

commit.message:
feat: Lint rule to detect ordering discrepancies on top level functions (#8996)

Closes #8977

The following notebook:

```python
import marimo

__generated_with = "unknown"
app = marimo.App(width="medium")

with app.setup:
    from dataclasses import dataclass


@app.cell
def _():
    # UI marked as reuable by ui but file cell decorator is not changing UNLESS moved to after referenced dataclasses ** 
    @dataclass
    class ParsedFile:
        "A parsed notebook file"
        imports: list[Const]
    return


@app.class_definition
@dataclass
class Const:
    "A constant assignment from a setup cell."
    name: str               # variable name
    src: str


if __name__ == "__main__":
    app.run()
```

Cannot serialize `ParsedFile` as a top level function because it depends
on `Const`. This is not well captured in the frontend and can lead to
user frustration and confusion. This lint rule is added to help mitigate
this footgun by suggesting a reordering. e.g.

```txt
error[reusable-definition-order]: Reusable definition 'uses_offset' depends on reusable definition(s) declared later in the notebook: `offset`
 --> tests/_lint/test_files/reusable_definition_order.py:7:1
   7 | @app.function
   8 | def uses_offset(x: int = offset()) -> int:
     |     ^
   9 |     return x + 1
hint: Move the referenced reusable definition(s) earlier in the notebook, before `uses_offset`.
```

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: pre-commit-ci[bot] <66853113+pre-commit-ci[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(jit): revert relaxed Smi guards and remove arguments trampolines

Revert three speculative Smi-or-Boolean guards back to strict
movsxd+cmp Smi checks (LoadGlobal, LoadKeyedGeneric, LoadNamedGeneric).
The relaxed cmp-against-JIT_UNDEFINED guard allows booleans through,
causing incorrect speculation when booleans appear in arithmetic.

Remove CreateMappedArguments/CreateUnmappedArguments/CreateRestParameter
trampoline arms, restoring the unconditional deopt fallthrough. The
trampoline enabled sloppy-mode functions in Maglev but they produce
wrong results for mutual-recursive even/odd patterns.

commit.message:
fix(jit): revert unsafe Phi coalescing and optimistic speculation

Two Maglev JIT correctness fixes:

1. regalloc: Revert relaxed Phi coalescing and accumulator chain extension.
   The relaxed condition computed an effective_phi_end excluding uses in
   exit blocks, which is unsafe when those blocks are reached from inside
   the loop body. The chain extension overwrote live Phi values by walking
   backward through arithmetic ops. Restores the strict coalescing check
   (phi_end > back_start + 1).

2. codegen: Remove optimistic Phi speculation in compute_smi_guarded.
   The optimistic path marked loop-carried Phis as smi_guarded when only
   one input was Smi and all consumers were arithmetic. But the downstream
   i64 overflow guards (jo) do not trigger when adding two heap handles
   (~0x4_0000_0000), causing raw tagged pointers to leak into arithmetic
   results. Restores strict all-inputs-in-set check.

commit.message:
Improve telemetry robustness and fix Linux device ID (#2405)

## Describe your changes

### Fix telemetry device ID on Linux/macOS
- Remove erroneous `@property` decorator on module-level
`get_telemetry_base_dir()` in `utils.py`. This turned the function into
a non-callable property descriptor, silently breaking device ID
retrieval on all Linux/macOS systems (Windows uses the registry and was
unaffected).
- Add graceful fallbacks when `HOME` is unset (common in containers):
tries `Path.home()`, then `/var/tmp`.
- Simplify `_store.py` with `mkdir(exist_ok=True)` to avoid race
conditions.

### Auto-disable telemetry in CI/CD environments
- Detect well-known CI env vars (`CI`, `TF_BUILD`, `GITHUB_ACTIONS`,
`JENKINS_URL`, `CODEBUILD_BUILD_ID`, `BUILDKITE`,
`SYSTEM_TEAMFOUNDATIONCOLLECTIONURI`) and auto-disable telemetry.
- CI check runs **before** heartbeat so no events are sent at all in
pipelines.
- User opt-out via `OLIVE_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1` runs **after** heartbeat
(intentional: heartbeat still fires for user telemetry).

### Fix robustness issues
- **Cache entries silently lost**: Add missing `json.loads()` after
base64 decode in `_read_cache_entries()` — without this, cached events
were always dropped on retry.
- **Crash when logger is None**: Guard all `self._logger` call sites
with None checks.
- **File handle leak**: Close file handle if lock acquisition fails in
`_ExclusiveFileLock`.
- **Negative HTTP timeout**: Clamp deadline calculation to `max(0.1,
...)` in exporter.
- **Session leak on init failure**: Close HTTP session if exporter init
fails post-session creation.
- **Transport retry**: Catch `RequestException` (not just
`ConnectionError`) on retry.
- **Cache flush race**: Use `wait_for_callbacks()` instead of single
snapshot check.
- **Crash-proof public API**: Wrap all public `Telemetry` methods in
`try/except`.

### Other fixes
- Fix `docs/Privacy.md`: correct `OLIVE_TELEMETRY_CACHE_PATH` →
`OLIVE_TELEMETRY_CACHE_DIR`, document as directory path, add CI
auto-detection docs.
- Add `# noqa: S110` comments to satisfy CodeQL empty-except findings.
- Exporter validates `options is not None` with clear error message.

## Checklist before requesting a review
- [ ] Add unit tests for this change.
- [x] Make sure all tests can pass.
- [x] Update documents if necessary.
- [x] Lint and apply fixes to your code by running `lintrunner -a`
- [x] Is this a user-facing change? If yes, give a description of this
change to be included in the release notes.

Description of change for release notes:
> Fix telemetry device ID on Linux/macOS, auto-disable telemetry in
CI/CD environments, and fix multiple robustness issues in the telemetry
cache.

---------
